data.title: JUST IN: Allegations of an Iran Crisis at Binance – Company Issues Statement
data.description:
US-based Fortune newspaper has highlighted a noteworthy development at Binance, one of the world’s largest cryptocurrency exchanges. According to the report, the company fired senior compliance inspectors who allegedly uncovered findings related to Iran-linked sanctions violations. In 2023, as part of an agreement with US authorities, Binance pleaded guilty to anti-money laundering (AML), know-your-customer (KYC), […] Continue Reading: JUST IN: Allegations of an Iran Crisis at Binance – Company Issues Statement

data.title: Bitcoin Price Rises as Open Interest Declines
data.description: Crypto KOL Ted posted on X that Bitcoin's price is increasing while open interest is decreasing. This trend suggests that the current rally may be driven by the closing of short positions.

data.title: Wealthy Clients' Tax Practices May Lead to IRS Attention
data.description: Wealthy individuals may face increased scrutiny from the IRS due to certain tax practices, according to elite advisers. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that these practices can potentially reduce tax refunds and attract unwanted attention from tax authorities. Advisers emphasize the importance of maintaining accurate and transparent tax records to avoid complications. They caution that even minor discrepancies can lead to audits, urging clients to adhere strictly to tax regulations. The focus on high-net-worth individuals comes as the IRS intensifies efforts to ensure compliance and address tax evasion. Experts recommend consulting with tax professionals to navigate complex tax codes and minimize risks.

data.title: Binance fired at least four internal investigators after reports surfaced that it had transacted with Iranian entities for over $1 billion.
data.description: Investigators from Binance's compliance team discovered that from March 2024 to August 2025, entities linked to Iran received over $1 billion through the exchange, with these transactions conducted via Tether on the Tron blockchain, potentially violating sanctions laws. Following the disclosure of these findings in an internal report, at least five people were dismissed starting in late 2025. Furthermore, at least four senior compliance personnel have left or been relieved of their duties in the past three months. Binance's Chief Compliance Officer, Noah Perlman, is scheduled to leave later this year, but his departure is unrelated to the investigators' dismissals. A Binance spokesperson stated that, according to policy, the company cannot comment on ongoing investigations and is committed to complying with all applicable sanctions laws and regulations in the markets in which it operates. Binance reportedly admitted in 2023 to violating anti-money laundering, customer verification laws, and sanctions regulations, agreeing to pay a $4.3 billion fine. (Fortune)

data.title: European Bank Stocks Plummet Amid AI Concerns
data.description:
On February 14, European bank stocks experienced a significant decline, erasing gains made earlier in the year. According to Jin10, the European bank stock index fell by 3%, with several major banks seeing notable drops in their share prices. Société Générale in Paris decreased by 5.3%, while German banking giants Commerzbank and Deutsche Bank fell by 5.2% and 3.6%, respectively. Spanish banks Santander and BBVA saw declines of 2.5% and 3.2%, and Italy's UniCredit dropped by 3.75%. Barclays also experienced a 2.3% decrease.

Joachim Klement from Panmure Liberum commented that banks have become the latest victims of concerns surrounding artificial intelligence competition. He noted that the theme of 'AI losers' has spread to the banking sector, with investors opting to sell first and ask questions later.

data.title: Grayscale Clarifies Use of 'ETF' Term for Exchange-Traded Products
data.description: Grayscale has clarified its use of the term 'ETF' to describe exchange-traded products, including spot crypto ETPs. Grayscale posted on X. These products are not registered under the Investment Company Act of 1940, meaning they do not adhere to the same regulations and protections as 40 Act registered ETFs and mutual funds.

data.title: Wall Street Firms Expand Crypto Teams Amid Market Volatility
data.description: Major financial institutions, including BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and CitiGroup, are increasing their hiring efforts to enhance expertise in digital assets. According to NS3.AI, this move comes as Wall Street progressively integrates cryptocurrency into traditional finance. Recent disclosures highlight Goldman Sachs' $2 billion exposure to crypto and BlackRock's acquisition of Uniswap governance tokens. Despite the cryptocurrency market losing half its value since October, these firms are investing in their crypto teams, indicating long-term confidence in blockchain technology and regulatory optimism under the current U.S. administration.

data.title: Launch of Cross-Chain Deposit Feature in TON Wallet Enhances User Experience
data.description: Wallet in Telegram has introduced a cross-chain deposit feature in its self-custodial TON Wallet, supported by MoonPay. According to NS3.AI, this functionality allows users to transfer stablecoins and other assets from major blockchains directly into the TON Wallet. The update simplifies the process of funding the TON Wallet by enabling deposits of USDC and USDT from Ethereum, Solana, TRON, BSC, Polygon, Arbitrum, and Base without requiring native TON assets initially. This seamless integration is designed to reduce entry barriers, encouraging broader adoption of the TON ecosystem and enhancing Telegram's position as a Web 3.0 access point.

data.title: Here is When 21Shares Will Pay Staking Distribution for its Solana ETF
data.description:
21Shares has confirmed a staking reward distribution for its 21Shares Solana ETF (TSOL), with shareholders set to receive $0. 316871 per share on February 17, 2026. The payment reflects rewards generated from the fund’s underlying Solana (SOL) staking activity, which allows the ETF to pass blockchain-based yield directly to investors
source: https://www.ethnews.com/here-is-when-21shares-will-pay-staking-distribution-for-its-solana-etf/

data.title: Significant Liquidation of Short Positions Following Bullish CPI Report
data.description: Crypto KOL Ted posted on X that $150 million in short positions have been liquidated in the past three hours. This development follows a bullish Consumer Price Index (CPI) report, which Ted suggested would lead to a hunt for short positions.

data.title: Tri-Party Custody Model Launched for Institutional SOL Staking
data.description: Anchorage Digital, Kamino, and Solana Company have partnered to launch a tri-party custody model aimed at institutional SOL staking. According to NS3.AI, this innovative framework allows institutions to engage in compliant staking while ensuring assets remain within regulated custody. The model facilitates access to on-chain lending and liquidity services without necessitating asset transfers outside the custody environment.

data.title: Hybrid Mortgage Loans Combine Bitcoin and Real Estate for Credit Access
data.description: Newmarket Capital is introducing a novel approach to mortgage loans by integrating Bitcoin and real estate as collateral. According to NS3.AI, this initiative aims to offer traditional credit access to borrowers without necessitating the liquidation of their cryptocurrency holdings. The strategy is designed to appeal to crypto-savvy individuals by combining income-generating properties with Bitcoin, thereby mitigating volatility risks while enabling participation in Bitcoin's potential appreciation. Although regulatory frameworks mandate that Bitcoin must be held on regulated exchanges for mortgage qualification, this development represents a significant advancement towards the mainstream adoption of cryptocurrency in real-world lending.

data.title: Altcoins Exhibit Mixed Performance Amid Volatile Market Conditions
data.description: Altcoins are displaying varied performance this week, with certain assets experiencing significant rallies while others encounter declines. According to NS3.AI, Pippin (PIPPIN) has surged over 200%, indicating breakout potential. Meanwhile, Aptos (APT) remains oversold and near its lows, and Kite (KITE) continues to reach new highs with strong momentum. Investors are advised to closely monitor support and resistance levels to effectively manage risk in these volatile conditions.

data.title: Scott Melker Predicts $COIN's Rise Amid Earnings Report
data.description: Scott Melker, host of The Wolf Of All Streets Podcast, posted on X, expressing optimism about $COIN's future. He noted that despite negative earnings perceptions, the company is poised to become one of the top 10 globally. $COIN is currently trading near its two-year low, presenting a potential buying opportunity according to Melker. He highlighted the weekly candle at support as a positive indicator and shared that he has increased his holdings.

data.title: U.S. Health Officials Jim O'Neill and Mike Stewart to Step Down
data.description: Jim O'Neill, the Deputy Secretary of the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, along with General Counsel Mike Stewart, are set to resign from their current positions. According to Jin10, this development comes amid ongoing changes within the department. The reasons for their departure have not been disclosed, and it remains unclear who will succeed them in their roles. This transition is part of a broader reshuffle within the U.S. health administration.
